streams.name: main
streams.focus: You are on the FOUNDATION stream (main branch). Work through F-01 to F-08 in order — these are sequential dependencies that unlock all other streams. Key deliverables: Go module init, Backend interface, Auditor interface, AuditEvent struct, dev (in-memory) backend, file audit sink, MultiAuditor, and adversarial tests for the Backend contract. Run: /coord status to see when downstream streams unlock.

streams.name: auth
streams.focus: You are on the AUTH stream (feature/auth-layer). The Backend and Auditor interfaces are defined on main — pull them. Work through A-01 to A-09 first: mTLS server setup, cert identity extraction, session token issuance and validation, token revocation, /auth/* HTTP handlers, and the agentkms-dev enroll CLI. A-10+ require OpenBao (coordinate with backend stream). Run: /coord next to see your next task.

streams.name: backend
streams.focus: You are on the BACKEND stream (feature/backend-abstraction). The Backend interface is defined on main — pull it. Implement B-01 first: the OpenBao/Vault Transit backend against that interface. B-01 unblocks: the API handlers stream (C-01+), LV-01 credential vending, and A-10 PKI integration. Write integration tests (B-02) against a local OpenBao instance started with agentkms-dev. Run: /coord next to see your next task.

streams.name: policy
streams.focus: You are on the POLICY stream (feature/policy-engine). Work through P-01 to P-04: define the rule schema, implement the YAML loader, build the policy evaluator, and enforce deny-by-default. CRITICAL: the test for P-04 must prove that an empty policy denies ALL operations. P-05+ integrate with OpenBao — coordinate with backend stream for B-01. Run: /coord next to see your next task.

streams.name: api
streams.focus: You are on the API HANDLERS stream (feature/api-handlers). You can stub the handlers now using the Backend interface from main. Full integration requires: A-04 (token middleware) from auth stream and B-01 (OpenBao) from backend stream. Build the handler structure, request validation, and policy check wiring first — leave the middleware and backend injection as clearly marked TODO stubs. C-06 (adversarial tests verifying no key material in responses) is P0 and must pass before merge. Run: /coord next to see your next task.

streams.name: pi-pkg
streams.focus: You are on the PI PACKAGE stream (feature/pi-package). This is the TypeScript thin-client extension — zero crypto logic here, only HTTP calls to AgentKMS. Read docs/architecture.md section 6 (Pi Integration Deep Dive) before starting. The critical path is: PI-01 scaffold, PI-02 HTTP client, PI-03 identity loader, PI-04 session_start hook, PI-05 provider override (the key injection mechanism), PI-06 before_provider_request hook. You can develop against the local agentkms-dev server. Run: /coord next to see your next task.

streams.name: local-dev
streams.focus: You are on the LOCAL DEV stream (feature/local-dev). Build the agentkms-dev server binary: single Go binary, in-memory backend (F-05), local dev CA + developer cert generation, and policy loaded from ~/.agentkms/dev-policy.yaml. The dev server must implement the full production API surface — no shortcuts, same mTLS + token lifecycle as production. Run: /coord next to see your next task.
